data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of fe57's premises (the fixtures+fake are grounded in real surfaces, not fictional, and flow through the PRODUCTION enrichment, not a parallel path):
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot exists (fetcher.py:480) and merges the enrichment maps into snapshot entries through the production path exactly as fe57 describes: comment field via client.get_comment_map (fetcher.py:402,413-414), plus issuelinks and parent enrichment (fetcher.py:380-472).
- The enrichment-method signatures fe57's FakeAcliClient targets are real and match: get_comment_map(self, project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289); get_issuelinks_map mirrors it (acli_graph.py:409). The existing production test fakes ALREADY swap a fake client with these signatures through the production fetch_snapshot path (e.g. tests/unit/.../mutate/test_comment_enrichment.py:196,206; test_link_sync.py:163,176) — confirming the injection seam fe57 relies on is real and in use.

File-impact disjointness: fe57 = tests/fixtures/jira/ (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py (new FakeAcliClient), scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py (new) + re-capture doc. DISJOINT from every sibling's edits — 8937 (_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh/test_roundtrip.py). fe57 only READS the production fetch/enrichment surface through the fake. Three siblings (8937, 59ef, 822a) declare depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's three files are net-new and disjoint from all siblings. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 59ef/8937/822a — a clean producer/consumer split over declared depends_on edges, not a shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937 (snapshot-entry schema targets exactly those unmassaged shapes), 59ef AC2 (an unmassaged fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes the return-shape contract), and 822a (round-trips those shapes). fe57 covers the INBOUND fetch/enrichment surface; the outbound labels_applied list-vs-count representation conflict tracked on the 33d0↔2359 pairing is outbound-internal and untouched by fe57 (fe57's inbound fixtures/fake neither assert nor depend on labels_applied).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation needing no sibling input, so it correctly declares no depends_on; the three downstream consumers correctly declare depends_on fe57. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc, and explicitly scopes OUT the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef), consistent with the roster. Distinct surface from every sibling (fixtures/fake vs schema vs telemetry vs applier-collapse vs probe).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures/fake must exist before the tests that consume them) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57 is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The fake adds NO shape massaging (AC2/AC4), so consumers receive exactly the production-fetcher shapes — this ENABLES rather than constrains siblings. The re-capture doc (AC3, "so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") guards the fixtures against change-detector rot, consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / parent "no net increase in brittle tests."
- Residual references — BENIGN. fe57 references only live inbound fetch/enrichment surfaces (all four enrichment methods + the fetch_snapshot merge points verified present). 33d0's deletion of the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier strands nothing fe57 references (fe57 drives the inbound fetch/enrichment path, not the outbound typed leaf). The secret-scrubbing/capture script targets live Jira behind a gate (no committed-secret hazard for siblings).

NOTE on the one real cross-child G4 finding in this epic (outbound labels_applied list-vs-count representation conflict): it is correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier-payload pairing. fe57's inbound fixtures/fake do not assert or depend on labels_applied, so it does not implicate this pairing.

NOTE on the parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause it is owned by 8937 — a G3 coverage matter, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency; not addressed here. (fe57's re-capture doc is self-scoped to its own fixtures, consistent with that, not a new G4 issue.)

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
